parentQuestion: A patient is given an IV saline solution that is isotonic to blood. Why is isoto...

scaffolds.q: Osmosis is the movement of ___ across a selectively permeable membrane.
scaffolds.explain: Osmosis is specifically the movement of WATER across a membrane, from high water concentration (low solute) to low water concentration (high solute). It's a special case of diffusion. Water follows solute gradients. Simple, elegant physics.

scaffolds.q: If a cell is placed in a hypotonic solution, water will move ___.
scaffolds.explain: HYPOtonic means 'lower solute concentration than the cell' — so there's MORE water outside (relative to solute). Water moves from high → low concentration, so it rushes INTO the cell. The cell swells. If it's an animal cell (no cell wall), it may burst. Beautiful and brutal physics.
